heading: Certain rural development investments by qualified telephone borrowers not treated as dividends or distributions
label: 926 Certain rural development investments by qualified telephone borrowers not treated as dividends or distributions
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , struck out “and the Governor of the telephone bank” after “The Secretary” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6602(b)(3)(A)Subsec. (a)(2). , struck out “or the Governor of the telephone bank” after “the Secretary”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6602(b)(3)(B)1994—Subsec. (a). substituted “Secretary” for “Administrator” in two places.Pub. L. 103–354
amendments: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , struck out “and the Governor of the telephone bank” after “The Secretary” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6602(b)(3)(A)Subsec. children.text: treat any amount invested by any qualified telephone borrower for any purpose described in (including any investment in, or extension of credit, guarantee, or advance made to, an affiliated company of the borrower, that is used by such company for such a purpose) as a dividend or distribution of capital to the extent that, immediately after such investment, the aggregate of such investments does not exceed ⅓ of the net worth of the borrower; orsection 2204b(c)(2) of this title
require a qualified telephone borrower to obtain the approval of the Secretary in order to make an investment described in paragraph (1).
to whom a telephone loan has been made or guaranteed under this chapter; and
whose net worth is at least 20 percent of the total assets of such person.
